user_message: Working on Humongous Insurance's security posture -- need you to draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 15:23 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Humongous Insurance

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 16:18 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 10:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance security posture

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-F trap (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-F failure mode (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-F -- Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email
